objective_statement: Describe basic data variable types (Boolean, numeric, string)
Describe basic structures used in data analytics (Tables, rows, columns, lists)
Describe data categories (Qualitative, quantitative, structured, unstructured, metadata, big data)

Import, store, and export data (Fundamental understanding of ETL extract transform and load processes, data manipulation tools including SQL R Python Microsoft Excel with aspects of Power Query, common data storage file formats)
Clean data (Purpose and common practices including handling NULL values, special characters, trimming spaces, inconsistent formatting, removing duplicates, imputing data, validating data)
Organize data (Purpose and common practices including sorting, filtering, slicing, transposing, appending, truncating)
Aggregate data (Purpose and common practices including grouping, joining/merging, summarizing, pivoting)
Describe and differentiate between types of data analysis (Descriptive, diagnostic, hypothesis testing, predictive, prescriptive)
Describe and differentiate between data aggregation and interpretation metrics (Sum, Max, Min, Count, Avg/Mean, Mode, Median, Std Dev)
Describe and differentiate between exploratory data analysis methods (Data relationships, drilling concepts, mining concepts including anomalies correlation patterns outliers)
Evaluate and explain results of data analyses (Trends, expected values, predictive model results, p-values, t-tests, regression analyses)
Define and describe the role of artificial intelligence in data analysis (AI, machine learning, algorithms and their application in analytics)
Report data (Effectively display information in tables and charts; explain when and why to disaggregate data)

Create visualizations from data (Identify visualization practices that minimize misinterpretation; select appropriate chart types for comparison trend part-to-whole relationship distribution correlation; box-whisker, scatter, bar, Sankey, histogram, pie, column charts)
Derive conclusions from data visualization (Translate visual representations into language; identify differences between claims and graphical representations)
Describe data privacy laws and best practices (GDPR, FERPA, HIPAA, IRB, PCI)
Describe best practices for responsible data handling (Methods of handling PII, securing data, protecting anonymity within small data sets)

Given a scenario, describe types of bias that affect collection and interpretation of data (Confirmation bias, human cognitive bias, motivational bias, sampling bias; visualization selection to avoid bias)
